mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production captures real output momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency in top INDPRO quintiles is not statistically above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency in UNRATE-triggered episodes is below 50% over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over the same period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is held at the zero lower bound, deepening negative real rates push investors out of cash and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation
failure_condition: Joint-score Rank IC between the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction and 20-day SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate regime, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal construction-led recovery and downstream demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency in triggered HOUST episodes under FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp 3-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above 0.4% signals impending monetary tightening fears that compress equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency in triggered CPIAUCSL episodes is below 52% over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal's mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate demand-supply synchronization without bottleneck pressure, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the RSAFS-INDPRO joint signal and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding household income and consumption capacity, supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Top quintile of PAYEMS changes fails to outperform the bottom quintile in 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% decline in housing starts over three months signals weakening construction activity and related sector drag that spills into broader demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency in triggered HOUST decline episodes is below 53% over any 3-year rolling window.
